content: Generalized Neighborhood Prompts can be integrated into the 2025 iteration of ICM curiosity mechanisms for autonomous agents to redefine what counts as 'novel' state for intrinsic reward calculation: an agent only gets an intrinsic reward if it encounters an observation that falls outside the generalized neighborhood of all previously seen states, rather than just comparing to individual raw observations. This creates a more human-like curiosity drive that prioritizes exploring entirely new conceptual domains instead of obsessing over trivial out-of-distribution edge variations of known states.
